title: GAT-12 Skipray Blastboat

Order: Force Heretic I: Remnant]]'' *[[Smuggler/Legends|Smugglers]] *[[Pirate/Legends|Pirates]] *Defense forces }} {{Quote|Skipray Blastboat here.|Unidentified [[Zann Consortium/Legends|Zann Consortium]] Pilots|}} The [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] '''GAT-12 Skipray Blastboat''' was, early in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the smallest [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]-capable combat [[Starship/Legends|starship]] in use by the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. ==Characteristics== [[File:Skipray_Blastboat.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A GAT-12h Skipray Blastboat]] While small enough to be carried aboard [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], its immense power output (produced by a [[Sarylcorp]] [[ViX multi-flux reactor]]), weaponry, and [[Deflector shield/Legends|shielding]] caused some registries to designate it as a capital ship itself. The most popular variant in the [[GAT Blastboat series|GAT series]], the GAT-12h, had a standard crew consisting of a pilot, copilot/[[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] operator, and two gunnery officers; however, the ship could be piloted by just one person. It was standard equipped with a Class Two [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] and a limited [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]] capable of storing four jump coordinates, and was stocked with sufficient [[Foodstuff/Legends|consumables]] for a month of independent operation. On short wings on the side of the cockpit it housed two [[Siep-Irol/Legends|Siep-Irol]] [["Omni-Vu" sensor pylon]]s. Two forward-swept [[Stabilizer/Legends|stabilizer fins]] branched out from a rotating assembly at the aft of the ship and housed two of the [[Mendarn Arms]] [[Dar-2 ion cannon]]s. The stabilizers were kept vertically in flight, with the ion cannons deployed above and below the rest of the ship; the fins rotated horizontally for landing, putting them on either side of the ship in a more traditional winged configuration.
[[File:GAT-12j-interior.png|thumb|left|200px|GAT-12j deck plan]]
A third Dar-2 ion cannon was housed in the nose of the ship, with a [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] launcher and [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] tube flanking it starboard and port, respectively. On the dorsal fuselage, a fire-linked pair of [[Senko Systems]] [[5000x2 "Tru-Lok" laser cannon]]s were mounted in a turret. While designed as a space-faring vessel, the ship actually performed better in atmospheric flight, with a (reported) top speed of 1,200 [[Kilometer/Legends|km]]/[[Standard hour/Legends|h]]; and its aerodynamic frame provided a better atmospheric maneuvering performance than all but high-performance [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]]. Its maneuvering performance was not as remarkable out of the atmosphere, and the ship often relied on swift first-strikes or coordination with other ships in space combat. ===Variants=== [[File:Skipray_schem.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Skipray [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]]]] In addition to the [[GAT-12h]], several other models were produced, including the GAT-12i, the GAT-12g (which replaced the concussion missile tube with a [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]]), the GAT-12j (which carried a larger payload while distributing gunnery duties more evenly), the GAT-12m (which had better [[Realspace/Legends|realspace]] and hyperspace performance, and thus was used as an interceptor and courier), and the GAT-12p (which had an upgraded sensor suite). A newer model, the [[Skipray 24r Blastboat]], saw service in the [[Jedi Coalition]] during the [[Second Galactic Civil War]].''[[Legacy of the Force: Invincible]]'' ==History== [[Republic Sienar Systems/Legends|Republic Sienar Systems]] designed the ship prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], with origins on the old [[Turbostorm-class gunship|''Turbostorm''-class gunship]]. Many of its systems were tested on [[Shobquix Yards]]' [[Toscan 8-Q starfighter|Toscan]] starfighters, modified by Sienar for the task as testbed gunships.''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]'' [[File:Skipray1.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A Skipray in orbit over a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]]] It was later sold primarily to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]]; the first GAT-12h Skipray Blastboats were released prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], being the smallest [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]-capable combat starship in use by the Imperial Navy (except for a few prototypes). The GAT-12h and its immediate successor (the [[GAT-12i]]) were more powerful than previous models that were slower and less combat-oriented. However, the Navy's tactical philosophy wasn't open to the idea of a hyperspace-capable capital-starfighter hybrid, and all but snubbed the ship. This led SFS to sell the ship on the open market. They proved to be a reliable design, going on to see extensive use in [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] planetary patrol forces and smuggling fleets for many years. GAT-12 blastboats were also used by [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|Imperial Customs]], Imperial [[Sector Ranger/Legends|Sector Rangers]] and local forces. Sub-models such as the [[GAT-12m]] and [[GAT-12p]] were used by the Empire. Those Imperial fleets that received Skiprays praised the ship highly, but the Skipray Blastboat ultimately gained its fame and reputation as a staple of corporate, [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]], [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]], and [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] fleets, as well as private use by individuals such as the former [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guard]] [[Kir Kanos]] (who used a modified GAT-12).
[[File:ImpLine.png|thumb|left|200px|An Imperial [[skirmish line]] with Skiprays and other vessels.]]
Though it was designed and marketed for the Empire, many of these ships fell into the hands of the [[Zann Consortium/Legends|Zann Consortium]] who added sensor jamming equipment to make their ships harder targets in space combat. During its service with the Consortium, its slow speed, coupled with heavy armor, made it an ideal fighter-bomber and it fulfilled a role similar to Imperial [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE Defender]] and Rebel [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing]] starfighters. The [[GAT-12g]] version was in use by the Imperial Navy some time after the Battle of Yavin.''[[The Far Orbit Project]]'' The [[GAT-12j]] upgrade was in production some months before the Battle of Endor and was released some weeks after the Imperial defeat. By [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]] the GAT-12j Skipray was the most common model in use. [[Talon Karrde]] used Skiprays for planetary defense; he lost two vessels in [[Myrkr/Legends|Myrkr]] due to an incident in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]] that involved [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. ==Behind the scenes== The mounted twin lasers are depicted as starfighter guns in some sources, but ''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]'' replaced them with a turbolaser.''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]'' ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox|content= [[File:Skipray_blastboat_landing_config.jpg|thumb|200px|A Skipray Blastboat in its landing configuration]]
